title: 10 best herbal teas for urinary calculus
intro_desc: Herbal teas for urinary calculus are a natural remedy that has been used to alleviate symptoms of kidney stones, also known as urinary calculi. These teas work by helping to flush out tiny stones and reduce inflammation in the kidneys and bladder, making them a comforting and effective way to manage this common condition. Examples include Uva Ursi tea, which helps to acidify urine and prevent stone formation, and Stinging Nettle tea, which reduces inflammation and eases pain. Drinking these teas can improve lives by providing relief from painful symptoms, reducing the need for surgical intervention, and promoting overall urinary tract health.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Burdock teas helps with urinary calculus because of its unique combination of antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and diuretic properties. The tea's saponins help break down and remove toxins from the kidneys, reducing the risk of stone formation. Additionally, burdock's flavonoids and phenolic acids have been shown to inhibit crystal growth and aggregation, making it an effective natural remedy for preventing and treating urinary stones.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties: Burdock tea's antioxidant compounds help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the urinary tract, which can contribute to the formation of kidney stones.
Diuretic properties: The diuretic effects of burdock tea increase urine production, which helps flush out toxins and excess minerals that can form into calculi (stones) in the kidneys or bladder.
Alkaline pH-balancing properties: Burdock tea has an alkalizing effect on the body, which can help neutralize acidic conditions in the urinary tract that contribute to the formation of kidney stones.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in inulin, a prebiotic fiber that helps soothe and flush out the urinary tract.
Barks: Contain sesquiterpenes, compounds that help reduce inflammation and alleviate pain associated with urinary calculi.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh roots of arctium lappa plants, washing them thoroughly under cold running water.
Chop the cleaned roots into small pieces, measuring out 20 grams for every liter of boiling water.
Bring the chopped roots and measured water to a rolling boil, reducing heat to simmer for 10 minutes.
Strain the tea mixture through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve, discarding solids and reserving liquid.
Allow the filtered tea to cool slightly before serving, consuming 1 liter every 8 hours.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion teas helps with urinary calculus because it is a natural diuretic that increases urine production, allowing for efficient removal of small stones or gravel-like particles from the kidneys and bladder. The tea's bitter properties also help to stimulate the gallbladder and liver, promoting healthy digestion and reducing inflammation in the urinary tract. Additionally, dandelion's anti-inflammatory compounds may help to reduce pain and discomfort associated with urinary calculi.
remedy_properties: Diuretic property: Dandelion tea increases urine production, which helps to flush out small stones and reduce the risk of further stone formation.
Anti-inflammatory property: Dandelion tea's anti-inflammatory compounds may help alleviate the pain and discomfort associated with urinary calculi by reducing inflammation in the urinary tract.
Antioxidant property: Dandelion tea's antioxidant properties may help protect against oxidative stress and damage to kidney cells, which can contribute to the development of urinary calculi.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots have been traditionally used in herbal medicine to support kidney function and relieve symptoms of urinary tract issues, making them a good addition to teas for urinary calculus.

Fruits: Dandelion fruits, also known as seed pods, are rich in antioxidants and flavonoids that can help soothe and calm the urinary system, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with kidney stones.
remedy_recipe: Measure out two teaspoons of dried taraxacum officinale herb and place it in a heat-resistant teapot or infuser.
Boil one cup of water and pour it over the herb, allowing it to steep for five to seven minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup using a piece of cheesecloth or a coffee filter to catch any loose particles.
Add honey to taste if desired and stir well before drinking, aiming for a dosage of one cup per day.
Prepare this tea twice daily, morning and evening, for at least two weeks to help alleviate urinary calculus symptoms.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Juniper teas helps with urinary calculus because it has natural diuretic properties that increase urine production, allowing for the efficient passage of stones through the urinary tract. The tea's anti-inflammatory compounds also help to reduce swelling and alleviate pain associated with kidney stones. Additionally, juniper berries have been shown to inhibit the growth of bacteria and fungi in the urinary tract, reducing the risk of infection and promoting a healthy environment for stone elimination.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory and analgesic effects: Juniper tea's anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties help alleviate pain, swelling, and discomfort associated with urinary calculi.
Diuretic activity: The diuretic properties of juniper tea increase urine production, which helps to flush out small stones or gravel that may be causing obstruction in the urinary tract.

Antioxidant and antimicrobial effects: Juniper's antioxidant and antimicrobial properties help protect against bacterial infections that can exacerbate urinary calculi and promote healing in the affected areas.
remedy_parts: Roots: Juniper roots contain a high concentration of volatile oils, which help to reduce inflammation and ease pain in the urinary tract.
Barks: The barks of the juniper tree are rich in flavonoids and terpenes, which have been shown to improve kidney function and reduce symptoms of urinary calculus.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh juniperus communis leaves and flowers at dawn or early morning when they are most potent.
Rinse the harvested plant material thoroughly with cold water to remove dirt and debris.
Combine one teaspoon of dried juniperus communis leaves and flowers with 250ml of boiling water in a teapot.
Steep the mixture for 10-15 minutes to allow the active compounds to infuse into the water.
Strain the tea through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth and serve warm or at room temperature after 30 minutes preparation time.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice teas helps with urinary calculus because it has been traditionally used to soothe and calm the urinary tract, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with kidney stones. The tea's anti-inflammatory compounds help to ease the passage of stones through the urinary tract, making them less likely to cause further blockages or complications. Additionally, licorice root has natural diuretic properties that increase urine flow, helping to flush out small stones and prevent their formation in the first place.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Licorice tea's antioxidants help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the kidneys, which can contribute to the formation of kidney stones.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds in licorice tea, such as glycyrrhizin, help reduce swelling and irritation in the urinary tract, making it easier for small stones to pass and preventing new stone formation.

Diuretic properties: Licorice tea's diuretic effects increase urine production, which helps flush out small stones and prevent recurrences by reducing the concentration of minerals in the urine that can form stones.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in glycyrrhizin, a compound that helps reduce inflammation and ease discomfort associated with urinary tract stones.
Barks: Contain flavonoids and saponins that work together to promote urination and help break down kidney stones.
remedy_recipe: Boil 2-3 grams of dried glycyrrhiza glabra roots in 250ml of water for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup and discard the solids after 5 minutes.
Add honey to taste if desired and mix well before serving.
Drink the tea three times a day, one hour before meals, for a period of six weeks.
Store any leftover tea in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to two days.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Corn teas helps with urinary calculus because of its ability to dissolve and expel small stones from the body. The herbs used in these teas, such as corn silk, uva ursi, and juniper berry, have natural diuretic properties that increase urine production, helping to flush out tiny stones and sediment. Additionally, these herbs contain compounds that can help break down mineral deposits and reduce inflammation in the urinary tract, making it an effective remedy for managing kidney stones and other types of urinary calculus.
remedy_properties: Dissolution of Stones: Corn silk tea is rich in mucilage, a thickening agent that helps to break down and dissolve urinary stones by coating them with a protective film, allowing for easier passage.
Antispasmodic Properties: The anthraquinones present in corn silk tea help to relax the muscles in the bladder and ureters, reducing spasms and pain associated with kidney stones.

Anti-inflammatory Effects: Corn silk tea contains flavonoids that have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce inflammation and swelling caused by urinary calculus, making it easier to pass the stone.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, which help reduce inflammation and ease pain associated with urinary tract issues.
Rhyzomes: Contain anti-inflammatory compounds that help soothe bladder discomfort and relieve symptoms of urinary calculi.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh maize ears or purchase dried corn cobs and grind them into a fine powder using a spice grinder.
Measure out 1 tablespoon of ground zea mays and combine it with 8 ounces of boiling water in a medium-sized saucepan.
Reduce heat to low and simmer for 10-15 minutes, allowing the herbal mixture to steep and release its beneficial properties.
Strain the tea mixture through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a large cup, discarding solids and retaining the liquid extract.
Allow the tea to cool for 5-10 minutes before consuming it within 30 minutes of preparation, taking 2-3 cups per day as needed.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Purple coneflower teas helps with urinary calculus because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling in the urinary tract, easing the passage of stones and preventing further growth. The tea's antioxidant compounds also work to neutralize free radicals, which can contribute to stone formation by damaging kidney tissue. Additionally, the anti-inflammatory properties may help alleviate pain and discomfort associated with passing calculi, making it a natural and soothing solution for those suffering from urinary calculus.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Purple coneflower tea contains antioxidants that help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the urinary tract, which can contribute to the formation of kidney stones.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds present in purple coneflower tea may help alleviate symptoms associated with urinary calculus, such as pain and discomfort, by reducing swelling and irritation in the urinary tract.
Antimicrobial properties: Purple coneflower tea has antimicrobial activity, which can help prevent bacterial infections that can exacerbate kidney stone formation and make it more difficult to pass.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in flavonoids and phenolic acids, which help reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms associated with urinary calculus.
Fruits: Containing anthraquinones, which have natural diuretic properties that help flush out small stones or gravel from the kidneys.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 grams of fresh or dried echinacea purpurea roots and flowers.
Cleanse the herbs by rinsing them gently under cold running water.
Combine the cleaned herbs with 250 milliliters of boiling distilled water.
Steep the mixture for 10-15 minutes in a glass jar covered with a lid.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a cup.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender teas helps with urinary calculus because of its natural diuretic properties, which increase urine production and help flush out small stones or gravel in the kidneys. The tea's antispasmodic qualities also alleviate pain and discomfort associated with kidney stone episodes. Additionally, lavender's anti-inflammatory compounds may reduce inflammation and swelling in the urinary tract, making it a soothing and effective herbal remedy for individuals suffering from urinary calculus.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Lavender tea helps reduce inflammation in the urinary tract, which can alleviate discomfort and pain associated with urinary calculi.
Antioxidant property: The antioxidants present in lavender tea help protect the kidneys from oxidative stress and damage caused by the presence of stones, reducing the risk of further complications.
Musculotropic property: Lavender tea's musculotropic properties help relax the smooth muscles in the urinary tract, which can aid in the passage of stones and reduce the likelihood of blockages.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of English lavender are often used to make medicinal teas as they possess anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms associated with urinary calculi.
Flowers: Lavender flowers have been traditionally used in herbal remedies for urinary issues, including the treatment of kidney stones, due to their ability to ease pain and discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Measure two teaspoons of dried lavandula angustifolia flowers for every cup of boiling water.
Heat one cup of filtered water in a saucepan over medium heat until it reaches a rolling boil.
Pour the boiling water over the measured lavandula angustifolia flowers and let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea using a fine-mesh strainer or cheesecloth into a teapot or mug to remove the herb particles.
Allow the tea to cool slightly before consumption, drinking 2-3 cups daily as needed.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Field horsetail teas helps with urinary calculus because of its unique composition, rich in silica, potassium, and other minerals. The tea's high water content makes it a natural diuretic, helping to flush out small stones and gravel from the kidneys and ureters. Additionally, field horsetail's antioxidant properties may reduce inflammation and prevent further stone formation by inhibiting crystal growth and aggregation. Regular consumption of this herbal tea can provide relief from urinary calculus symptoms and promote overall kidney health.
remedy_properties: Silica content: Horsetail tea contains high amounts of silica, which helps to prevent the formation of kidney stones by reducing the concentration of calcium oxalate in the urine.

Anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties: The antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds present in horsetail tea may help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the urinary tract, which can contribute to the development of kidney stones.

Urinary antiseptic properties: Horsetail tea has been shown to have antibacterial and antifungal properties, which can help prevent infections that can exacerbate urinary calculus or make it more difficult to pass.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the field horsetail contain a high concentration of silica, which helps to dissolve and remove kidney stones.
Rhizomes: The rhizomes of the field horsetail have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce pain and swelling associated with urinary calculi.
remedy_recipe: Measure 2 teaspoons of dried equisetum arvense herb into a clean glass cup or teapot.
Heat 8 ounces of boiling water in the cup or teapot and let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea mixture through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into another clean cup.
Discard the solids and let the tea cool to room temperature before refrigerating it for up to 24 hours.
Consume 1 cup of the cooled tea three times a day, 30 minutes before meals for optimal results.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Psyllium teas helps with urinary calculus because its soluble fiber content absorbs excess water in the intestine, reducing stool bulk and promoting regular bowel movements. This alleviates pressure on the kidneys, allowing them to function more efficiently in eliminating calcium oxalate stones from the body. The tea's mucilaginous properties also help soften and dislodge small stones, facilitating their passage through the urinary tract and preventing blockages. As a result, herbal psyllium teas can be an effective natural remedy for managing urinary calculus symptoms and promoting overall kidney health.
remedy_properties: Solvent and binding agent: Psyllium husk acts as a solvent, helping to dissolve and flush out small calculi (kidney stones) from the urinary tract, while its binding properties prevent larger stones from forming.

Anti-inflammatory and anti-edematous properties: The bioactive compounds in psyllium tea have been shown to reduce inflammation and swelling in the urinary tract, which can help alleviate symptoms associated with kidney stone passage, such as pain and bleeding.

Cholesteric and lithotriptic properties: Psyllium tea has been found to stimulate bile production and improve gallbladder function, which can aid in dissolving and removing calculi from the urinary system, while its lithotriptic properties help break down and fragment stones.
remedy_parts: Roots: Psyllium roots are rich in mucilage, a thick, protective substance that helps soothe and lubricate the urinary tract, making it an effective ingredient for treating urinary calculus.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzome of psyllium plant contains compounds that have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling and alleviate discomfort caused by urinary calculi.
remedy_recipe: Measure one teaspoon of dried plantago ovata leaves and flowers into a heat-resistant cup or teapot.
Heat fresh, filtered water to a rolling boil in a separate pot or kettle and pour over the herb mixture.
Allow the tea to steep for 5-7 minutes to release the active compounds and flavonoids.
Strain the liquid through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into another cup to remove the solids.
Drink the warm tea immediately, taking up to three cups per day as needed.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon verbena teas helps with urinary calculus because its high concentration of antioxidants, flavonoids, and essential oils provides a natural solution for relieving kidney stone symptoms. The tea's anti-inflammatory properties can help reduce swelling and pain associated with the condition, while its diuretic effects promote increased urine production to flush out small stones and prevent new ones from forming. Additionally, lemon verbena's antimicrobial properties may also help combat bacterial infections that often accompany urinary calculus.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Lemon verbena's antioxidant properties can help reduce oxidative stress in the body, which may contribute to the formation of kidney stones and other urinary issues, by neutralizing free radicals and protecting cells from damage.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds present in lemon verbena tea, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, may help alleviate inflammation and swelling associated with urinary calculus, reducing pain and discomfort.

Diuretic properties: Lemon verbena's diuretic effects can increase urine production, helping to flush out small kidney stones or reduce the size of larger ones by increasing the flow of fluids through the kidneys and urinary tract.
remedy_parts: Roots: Lemon verbena roots have been traditionally used to treat kidney stones and other urinary issues due to their anti-inflammatory properties.
Barks: The barks of the lemon verbena plant are rich in flavonoids, which help to reduce inflammation and alleviate pain associated with urinary calculi.
remedy_recipe: Measure one teaspoon of dried verbena officinalis flowers into a tea infuser or heat-resistant cup.
Pour one cup of boiling water over the herb and let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into a separate container using a tea strainer or cheesecloth to remove the solids.
Add one tablespoon of honey to sweeten the tea and stir well to combine.
Let the tea cool slightly before drinking, allowing the flavors to meld together for 2-3 minutes.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat urinary calculus with herbal teas is by combining certain herbs that have natural diuretic, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties. A combination of dandelion root, juniper berries, and uva ursi tea can help increase urine flow, reduce stone size, and alleviate symptoms such as pain and burning during urination.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to urinary calculus that are treated with herbal teas are kidney stones, gallstones, and gout. Herbs like stinging nettle, dandelion root, and juniper berries help dissolve and remove these stone-forming substances from the body, reducing pain and inflammation. Other herbs like turmeric and ginger also provide anti-inflammatory properties to soothe the affected areas. These herbal teas can be consumed as a preventive measure or used in combination with traditional treatment options for better results.
